title: catalog_edit (v1)
description: A surgical edit to a canonical catalog file (e.g. aporia/mathematics/tensor_open_problems_v1.md). Tier-2 mandatory if any catalog row is stale. before/after strings are verbatim; ingest_substrate_blocks.py refuses to apply if the before string is not unique in the target file (anti-fabrication enforcement).

catalog_file.title: Path to the catalog file (repo-relative)
catalog_file.description: Repo-relative path. Validator confirms file exists. Initial pilot scope: only aporia/mathematics/tensor_open_problems_v1.md is accepted; future catalogs add to the enum.

entry_id.description: T#NN entry being edited. Cross-reference checked at validate-time: the validator confirms entry_id exists in the target catalog_file.

field.title: Field within the entry being edited
field.description: Free-form descriptor of which part of the entry is changing. Examples: 'status_paragraph', 'attack_vectors', 'authors', 'bound', 'citation_references'. Reviewer interprets; the verbatim before/after strings are what actually drives the edit.

before.title: Verbatim current text in the catalog
before.description: EXACT current text from the catalog file. ingest_substrate_blocks.py performs a string-replace using this as the search key; if the before string does not appear unchanged in the target file, the ingest refuses (anti-fabrication: Gemini cannot edit text it didn't actually find). Whitespace is significant.

after.title: Verbatim replacement text
after.description: Exact replacement text. May include inline citations using the standard catalog format. Should preserve catalog conventions (paragraph wrapping, citation style).

citation.description: Primary citation motivating the edit. Required even for stylistic edits (the substrate's epistemic-explicitness discipline).

edit_type.title: Edit propagation discipline
edit_type.description: PROPAGATE = well-confirmed edit; ingestable directly. VERIFY-LIVE = needs second-pass before catalog write (e.g. an unverified frontier claim).

motivation.title: Why this edit is required now
motivation.description: One sentence. Examples: 'omega upper bound updated 2.371552 -> 2.371339 (Vassilevska Williams 2024)', 'tensor isomorphism complexity refined: GI-hard not just suspected'.

associated_anchor_pins.title: Anti-anchor IDs related to this edit
associated_anchor_pins.description: When a catalog row's update relates to an existing anti-anchor (e.g. correcting a stale 'X solved Y' claim that's pinned in AA-NNN), name the anchors here. Cross-reference checked at validate-time.

examples.before: The infimum of τ such that two n×n matrices can be multiplied in O(n^τ) operations. Current bounds 2 ≤ ω ≤ 2.371552 (Williams; Duan-Wu-Zhou 2023; Vassilevska Williams-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024).
examples.after: The infimum of τ such that two n×n matrices can be multiplied in O(n^τ) operations. Current bounds 2 ≤ ω ≤ 2.371339 (Vassilevska Williams-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024 update; supersedes 2.371552 from Duan-Wu-Zhou 2023).
examples.motivation: omega upper bound updated 2.371552 -> 2.371339 from VWXXZ 2024 final preprint; current text references DWZ 2023 only.
